records.ety: [OE. proclamen, L. proclamare; pro before, forward + clamare to call or cry out: cf. F. proclamer. See Claim.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Proclaimed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Proclaiming.]

defs: To make known by public announcement; to give wide publicity to; to publish abroad; to promulgate; to declare; as, to proclaim war or peace.
quotes: To proclaim liberty to the captives.
For the apparel oft proclaims the man.
Throughout the host proclaim A solemn council forthwith to be held.
